source: Palette after Johannes Itten's twelve-part color wheel (Farbkreis) with the white and black poles of his color sphere; combinations composed after the seven color contrasts, the geometric chords, and the season studies in 'Kunst der Farbe' (The Art of Color, 1961) and his Bauhaus preliminary course teaching. Hues are interpretive approximations of the printed wheel.

combinations.name: First Triad
combinations.note: The contrast of pure hue at its strongest: the undiluted primaries, an equilateral chord on the twelve-part wheel.

combinations.name: Second Triad
combinations.note: The secondary chord - orange, violet, green - the same equilateral triangle rotated one step.

combinations.name: Third Triad
combinations.note: The first tertiary chord: yellow-orange, red-violet, blue-green.

combinations.name: Fourth Triad
combinations.note: The second tertiary chord: red-orange, blue-violet, yellow-green.

combinations.name: Yellow and Violet
combinations.note: The complementary pair that is also the strongest light-dark contrast among the hues, set on neutral ground.

combinations.name: Blue and Orange
combinations.note: The complementary pair that carries the cold-warm contrast, each demanding the other.

combinations.name: Red and Green
combinations.note: The complementary pair of equal brilliance - the simultaneous contrast at rest.

combinations.name: Cold-Warm Poles
combinations.note: Itten's temperature poles: red-orange, the warmest of all hues, against blue-green, the coldest.

combinations.name: Spring
combinations.note: The season studies: youthful, light, yellow-leaning - spring announced in bright greens and warmed whites.

combinations.name: Summer
combinations.note: The season studies: dense and saturated, foliage at full weight with warmth glowing inside it.

combinations.name: Autumn
combinations.note: The season studies: the year burning down through oranges and reds, violet waiting in the shadows.

combinations.name: Winter
combinations.note: The season studies: cold, sparse, withdrawn - blues against white ground and black bareness.

combinations.name: The Warm Half
combinations.note: Five steps along the warm half of the wheel, yellow to red - a peripheral scale of rising temperature.

combinations.name: The Cold Half
combinations.note: Five steps along the cold half of the wheel, green to violet - the descending scale of temperature.
